text: HRV-guided training produces a small but significant improvement in VO2max compared to traditional predefined training in endurance athletes, with an effect size of 0.402.
The difference in VO2max improvement between HRV-guided and control training groups, while both improved significantly, favored HRV-guided training with an effect size of 0.187.
Amateur-level endurance athletes benefit more from HRV-guided training for VO2max improvement than higher-level athletes.
Female endurance athletes show greater VO2max improvements from HRV-guided training compared to male athletes.
The effectiveness of HRV-guided training on VO2max is moderated by athlete level and sex, with less trained and female athletes showing the greatest benefits.
Both HRV-guided and traditional predefined training programs significantly improve VO2max in endurance athletes, but HRV-guided approaches yield a statistically greater improvement.
specific_value: p < 0.0001 for both groups; p < 0.0001 for between-group difference
